entities.name: GENOMICS TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies used to analyze the genome to improve crops

entities.name: BREEDING METHODS
entities.description: Techniques used to develop improved crop varieties

entities.name: BREEDER'S EQUATION
entities.description: A mathematical formula used to estimate the rate of genetic gain per unit of time

entities.name: CLIMATE RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand and recover from climate-related stresses

entities.name: NUTRITION TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics of a crop that determine its nutritional value

entities.name: GENETIC GAIN
entities.description: The increase in the average value of a trait in a population over time due to selection

entities.name: ALLELES
entities.description: Alternative forms of a gene that can influence traits

entities.name: HAPLOTYPES
entities.description: A group of alleles inherited together from a single parent

entities.name: SELECTION INTENSITY
entities.description: The proportion of the population selected for the next generation

entities.name: STATISTICAL METHODS
entities.description: Mathematical frameworks used for experimental design and trait estimation

entities.name: HIGH-THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING
entities.description: Rapid and automated measurement of plant traits

entities.name: HERITABILITY
entities.description: The proportion of phenotypic variation attributable to genetic variation

entities.name: BREEDING CYCLE TIME
entities.description: The time taken to complete one cycle of selection and breeding

entities.name: SPEED BREEDING
entities.description: Techniques used to accelerate the generation time of plants

entities.name: CROP
entities.description: Plants grown for food, fiber, or other economic purposes
